rationale: The citing paper uses Aribarg et al. (2002) in its core literature-positioning on joint family decision making, not just as a peripheral background mention. It explicitly points to prior work including Aribarg et al. as assuming a Harsanyi-style influence aggregation and states that this assumption should be empirically validated. The citing study then tests Harsanyi versus MIN/MAX heuristics and reports heterogeneity across families, which functionally qualifies the earlier assumption.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Aribarg et al. (2002) as prior joint decision-making work that assumes a specific group utility rule (Harsanyi) and uses it as a benchmark in theory framing. It then refines that line by empirically testing alternative heuristics and showing the assumption is only partially dominant across families.
